rationale: The cited 2009 paper and the citing 2017 paper are both in physician-level pharmaceutical detailing and explicitly model strategic competitive reactions alongside demand, with closely aligned constructs (e.g., competitive detailing, firm strategic behavior, joint demand/marketing modeling). Given this strong methodological/domain overlap and the citing paper’s stated extension of competitive-reactions work for ex ante policy prediction, this citation is plausibly foundational or a direct comparison point rather than an incidental mention.
